title: Billing banner copy update behind existing flag
artifact_text:
diff --git a/apps/billing-web/src/banner.tsx b/apps/billing-web/src/banner.tsx
- "Your payment is overdue."
+ "Payment overdue. Update billing details to avoid interruption."
Note: existing LaunchDarkly flag `billing_banner_v2` remains in place.
expected_blast_radius: Billing web overdue banner only; no API, schema, or infra impact in evidence.
expected_key_concerns: Confirm the flag already exists and the default targeting is unchanged.
Verify the copy change does not require localization or legal review.
expected_missing_info: Planned rollout timing or cohort is not shown.
expected_rollback_notes: Disable the existing flag or revert the copy commit.
expected_exec_summary: Low-risk content change limited to one web surface and easily reversible.

title: PDF worker memory limit increase
artifact_text:
kubernetes/deploy/pdf-worker.yaml
resources:
  limits:
-   memory: "512Mi"
+   memory: "768Mi"
Replicas unchanged: 6
Reason: repeated OOMKilled during month-end exports
expected_blast_radius: Async PDF export workers in production; no direct request-path or data-model change.
expected_key_concerns: Check cluster headroom before increasing memory across all replicas.
Monitor queue depth and pod restarts after rollout.
expected_missing_info: Current node capacity and headroom are not included.
expected_rollback_notes: Restore the previous limit and redeploy the worker if capacity pressure increases.
expected_exec_summary: Low-risk infrastructure tuning for a non-customer-facing worker, with localized capacity concerns.

title: Checkout one-page experience feature flag rollout
artifact_text:
CHG-4821
Enable `checkout_onepage_v2` in prod:
- employees: 100%
- us-east customers: 5% -> 25% after 2 hours
Scope: checkout-web only
Success metric: checkout completion rate
Rollback: set flag to 0%
expected_blast_radius: Checkout web traffic in the initial customer cohort; conversion and payment initiation behavior may shift.
expected_key_concerns: Customer-facing checkout changes can affect abandonment and payment errors.
The move from 5% to 25% needs clear stop/go guardrails.
expected_missing_info: No explicit owner or approval gate for advancing rollout stages.
No evidence that backend APIs are unchanged.
expected_rollback_notes: Return the flag to 0% or the prior cohort immediately if conversion or payment metrics regress.
expected_exec_summary: Medium-risk rollout on a critical customer journey, mitigated by flag control and staged exposure.

title: Concurrent index migration on orders table
expected_blast_radius: Orders primary and replicas; possible write amplification, lock pressure, and replica lag during build.
expected_key_concerns: Concurrent index creation still adds load on large production tables.
Replication lag and write latency should be watched during execution.
expected_missing_info: Table size and expected migration duration are not provided.
Execution window and on-call coverage are not shown.
expected_rollback_notes: Drop the new index if write latency or replica lag becomes unacceptable.
expected_exec_summary: Medium-risk additive database migration: safer than a blocking index, but still material on a production orders table.

title: JWT library major upgrade for security advisory
artifact_text:
package.json
- "jsonwebtoken": "^8.5.1"
+ "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2"
Reason: token verification bypass advisory

auth-api/src/verify.ts
- jwt.verify(token, key)
+ jwt.verify(token, key, { algorithms: ["RS256"] })
expected_blast_radius: Auth API and any application depending on issued or verified tokens; login and service auth may be affected.
expected_key_concerns: Major-version auth library changes can reject tokens that previously passed.
A security fix is desirable, but auth regressions have broad user impact.
expected_missing_info: Compatibility testing for existing token issuers and session refresh is not shown.
There is no stated plan for existing sessions if verification behavior changes.
expected_rollback_notes: Revert the dependency only with explicit risk acceptance for the advisory, and document any compensating controls.
expected_exec_summary: Medium-risk security upgrade on a critical auth path: needed for exposure reduction, but behavior changes can break login flows.

title: Tighten production application security group ingress
artifact_text:
# module.app_sg.aws_security_group_rule.ingress_https will be replaced
- cidr_blocks     = ["10.0.0.0/8"]
+ security_groups = ["sg-alb-prod", "sg-bastion-prod"]
Plan: 2 to change, 0 to add, 0 to destroy
Applies to prod app subnet in us-east-1
expected_blast_radius: Services in the prod app subnet in us-east-1; missed allowlist entries can cause broad connectivity loss.
expected_key_concerns: Any omitted upstream source can break customer traffic or operational access.
Health checks, internal callers, and break-glass paths all need validation.
expected_missing_info: The complete inventory of allowed sources is not in evidence.
No non-prod validation or canary test results are included.
expected_rollback_notes: Re-apply the prior Terraform plan or restore the previous rule set immediately if target health or connection success drops.
expected_exec_summary: High-risk network control change with potentially wide blast radius if the new allowlist is incomplete.

title: Payments gateway endpoint env var cutover
artifact_text:
Deployment env
- PAYMENTS_API_BASE_URL=https://payments.internal/v1
+ PAYMENTS_API_BASE_URL=https://payments-v2.internal/v1
Services: checkout-api, refund-worker
Reason: cut over to new gateway
No code diff attached
expected_blast_radius: Checkout and refund payment calls; charge, capture, and refund flows depend on the new endpoint behaving identically.
expected_key_concerns: A single env var redirects critical payment traffic and can change auth, schemas, or timeout behavior.
No code diff means contract assumptions and integration coverage are unclear.
expected_missing_info: TLS/auth compatibility with the new gateway is not shown.
No staged rollout or shadow-test evidence is included.
It is unclear whether the old endpoint stays available for rollback.
expected_rollback_notes: Restore the previous env var and redeploy or restart the affected services while keeping the old gateway path available.
expected_exec_summary: High-risk config-only change because it reroutes critical payment traffic without attached compatibility evidence.

title: Drop legacy customer tier column
artifact_text:
CHG-4894
Migration `20260320_drop_customer_legacy_tier.sql`
ALTER TABLE customers DROP COLUMN legacy_tier;
Justification: field unused after loyalty rewrite
Execution window: Sat 22:00 CT
Rollback: restore from snapshot if needed
expected_blast_radius: Customers table readers, exports, and background jobs still referencing the dropped column.
expected_key_concerns: This is a destructive schema change with no instant rollback.
Hidden downstream consumers such as ETL jobs and ad hoc reports are a common failure point.
expected_missing_info: No dependency scan or evidence that all consumers stopped reading the column.
Restore timing and tested recovery steps are not shown.
expected_rollback_notes: Treat rollback as recovery, not a quick revert; prefer a tested snapshot restore or emergency re-add plus backfill plan.
expected_exec_summary: High-risk destructive database migration that should not proceed without stronger dependency and recovery evidence.

title: Blue-green cutover for orders API gateway
artifact_text:
Release 2026.03.18
Switch `orders-api` production traffic from gateway-blue to gateway-green at 50% for 30 min, then 100%.
No app code changes in this release.
Health checks: p95 latency, 5xx rate, order submit success
expected_blast_radius: Orders API production traffic during the cutover; customer order submission is in scope.
expected_key_concerns: Traffic-management changes can surface config drift even without code changes.
The jump from 50% to 100% needs explicit rollback thresholds.
expected_missing_info: No evidence that gateway-green has matched certificates and upstream integrations.
Rollback trigger thresholds are not specified.
expected_rollback_notes: Shift traffic back to gateway-blue if latency, 5xx rate, or order success degrades.
expected_exec_summary: Medium-risk rollout on a core API, mitigated by blue-green control but still sensitive because it changes live traffic routing.

title: Reporting worker readiness probe delay increase
artifact_text:
k8s/deployments/reporting-worker.yaml
- initialDelaySeconds: 10
+ initialDelaySeconds: 30
Reason: container warms JVM before accepting work
Only `reporting-worker` deployment affected
expected_blast_radius: Reporting-worker startup behavior only; no steady-state request or schema impact in evidence.
expected_key_concerns: Longer startup time may slow replacements during rollout.
Queue backlog should be checked if multiple pods restart together.
expected_missing_info: Deployment surge and unavailable settings are not shown.
expected_rollback_notes: Restore the previous probe delay and redeploy if rollout speed or worker availability regresses.
expected_exec_summary: Low-risk infrastructure tuning scoped to one async worker, with minor rollout-speed considerations.

title: Vague production API performance tuning request
artifact_text:
CHG-4910
"Optimize production API performance before customer summit."
Notes: "May update caching, DB queries, and autoscaling if needed."
Services: TBD
Rollout / rollback: TBD
expected_blast_radius: Unknown; the text implies production-facing changes across multiple layers, but the actual systems are not identified.
expected_key_concerns: The change scope is too vague for a defensible risk tier.
Possible impact spans application, database, cache, and autoscaling if all hinted areas are touched.
expected_missing_info: Exact systems, environments, and artifacts changing.
Concrete rollout plan, rollback plan, and success metrics.
Whether the work is one change or several separate changes.
expected_rollback_notes: Rollback cannot be evaluated responsibly until the actual change set is specified.
expected_exec_summary: Risk should remain unknown because the evidence does not describe a concrete production change.

title: Shared cache layer rollout with insufficient evidence
artifact_text:
Release note draft:
"Introduce new shared cache layer for core services next sprint to reduce latency."
No service list, topology, TTL policy, tenancy model, or migration plan included.
expected_blast_radius: Unknown shared-service scope; could affect multiple applications, but the artifact does not say which ones.
expected_key_concerns: A shared cache can create cross-service failures or stale-data issues.
There is not enough implementation detail to rate the change credibly.
expected_missing_info: Which services, regions, and tenants are in scope.
Cache invalidation, fallback, and bypass behavior.
Rollout sequencing and rollback mechanics.
expected_rollback_notes: Require an explicit disable or bypass plan before rollout; current evidence is insufficient.
expected_exec_summary: Unknown risk: potentially broad infrastructure impact, but the artifact lacks the details needed for a reliable assessment.
